title: Leandra glandulifera
intro: Leandra glandulifera is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leandra glandulifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Leandra glandulifera is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
